basics.name: Alex Baker
basics.label: Undergraduate Researcher in Computational Astrophysics
basics.summary: Undergraduate physics student at Université Laval working at the intersection of astrophysics, machine learning, and scientific computing. Developing interpretable, physically grounded pipelines for large astronomical surveys. Currently preparing for graduate studies in computational astrophysics.

courses: PHY-3500 — Data Analysis and Machine Learning (course article on interpretable ML for stellar spectral classification, supervised by Prof. Antoine Allard)

work.name: Département de physique, de génie physique et d'optique, Université Laval
work.summary: Academic support for undergraduate students in mechanics and special relativity.
highlights: Explanation of complex physical concepts and correction of mathematical problems.

projects.name: AstroSpectro
projects.summary: Interpretable ML pipeline for stellar spectral classification on LAMOST DR5 × Gaia DR3.
highlights: End-to-end pipeline on 43,000 spectra with 183 physics-based spectroscopic descriptors, XGBoost classifier (ROC-AUC 0.964, 87% balanced accuracy), PCA, UMAP, autoencoder, and SHAP interpretability.
Key finding: SHAP analysis reveals metallicity-sensitive features (Ca II H&K, Mg b) systematically outrank temperature-proxy features (Balmer series) in the learned classification structure.
Manuscript in preparation for Research Notes of the AAS (RNAAS). 85+ experiment runs tracked with Weights & Biases.

projects.name: AstroVision
projects.summary: Deep learning pipeline for galaxy morphological classification with multi-modal fusion and uncertainty quantification.
highlights: Late-fusion classifier combining DINOv2 visual features, non-parametric morphometrics (CAS, Gini, M20), and SDSS photometry on Galaxy10 DECaLS (17,736 images, 10 classes).
86.3% balanced accuracy. MC Dropout uncertainty quantification, conformal prediction, and attention-guided morphometry.

projects.name: ξ Dark Energy · BAO MCMC
projects.summary: Bayesian MCMC analysis comparing dark energy models using Pantheon+SH0ES and DESI DR2 BAO data.
highlights: Compares ΛCDM, CPL, and oscillating dark energy (Ξosc) parameterisations using emcee. AIC/BIC model comparison.
Reproducible inference pipeline with fully documented limitations. Released as v0.1.0-exploratory.

projects.name: MOSS System — Co-Inventor (Provisional Patent)
projects.summary: Co-inventor of the Modular Orchestrated Storage System, a persistent memory architecture for large language models.
highlights: Designed the orchestration API and developed Python prototypes.
Active valorisation process with Axelys and BLUM, Université Laval.

publications.name: Metallicity Indicators Dominate Stellar Spectral Classification: A SHAP Interpretability Analysis of LAMOST DR5 Spectra
Réduction de dimension et interprétabilité appliquées aux spectres stellaires LAMOST DR5
publications.summary: Course report and symposium contribution. Supervised by Prof. Antoine Allard. Co-authors: Justine Jean, Nérimantas Caillat.
